name: portfolio-tracker
title: Portfolio Tracker
description: Track value of your Stocks, ETFs, Properties, Cash, Debt and Crypto with automatic price updates over time to see your net worth
author: filipawaits

commands.name: portfolio
commands.title: Portfolio Tracker
commands.description: Track your investment portfolio and net worth across multiple accounts

commands.name: search-investments
commands.title: Search Investments
commands.subtitle: Portfolio Tracker
commands.description: Search for stocks ETFs, and funds to add to your portfolio

commands.name: fire
commands.title: FIRE Dashboard
commands.subtitle: Portfolio Tracker
commands.description: Financial Independence, Retire Early — track your progress to financial freedom

tools.name: get-portfolio-summary
tools.title: Get Portfolio Summary
tools.description: Returns a complete overview of the user's investment portfolio including all accounts, positions, asset type allocation, currencies held, and position counts. Use this to answer questions about portfolio composition, holdings, and asset allocation.

tools.name: get-account-details
tools.title: Get Account Details
tools.description: Returns detailed information about a specific portfolio account identified by name. Includes all positions within that account with their full details. Use when the user asks about a specific account.

tools.name: get-position-details
tools.title: Get Position Details
tools.description: Returns detailed information about a specific position identified by name or Yahoo Finance symbol. Searches across all accounts. Use when the user asks about a specific holding or investment.

tools.name: get-debt-summary
tools.title: Get Debt Summary
tools.description: Returns a focused summary of all debt positions including balances, APR, monthly repayments, and repayment status. Use when the user asks about their debts, total debt, or repayment progress.

tools.name: get-fire-projection
tools.title: Get FIRE Projection
tools.description: Returns the user's FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early) projection including settings, contributions, and timeline. Use when the user asks about their FIRE number, retirement timeline, or current FIRE trajectory.

tools.name: simulate-fire-scenario
tools.title: Simulate FIRE Scenario
tools.description: Runs a custom FIRE projection with user-specified overrides for what-if analysis. Compares the scenario against the user's current baseline settings. Use when the user asks hypothetical questions like 'What if I increase contributions?' or 'What if growth drops to 5%?'.

ai.instructions: Portfolio Tracker is a personal finance extension that tracks stocks, ETFs, properties, cash, and debt across multiple accounts. All data is stored locally — there are no external accounts or servers. Prices refresh daily via Yahoo Finance. The FIRE Dashboard projects a path to financial independence using compound growth with inflation-adjusted (real) returns. When answering questions: always use the tools to fetch current data rather than assuming values. Portfolio values shown by tools may not include live market prices (tools read from local storage, not live APIs), so note this when discussing exact portfolio value. For FIRE projections, the real growth rate = (nominal growth - inflation) / 100. Contributions are monthly amounts that compound with half-year approximation. The FIRE number = annual spending × (100 / withdrawal rate). Debt balances are subtracted from portfolio value to produce net worth. Property positions use UK HPI data for appreciation. When the user mentions currencies, note that holdings in different currencies are converted to the base currency (configurable in preferences, default GBP) using live FX rates.

  Working Days to FIRE: 3,150

evals.input: @portfolio-tracker What if I increase my contributions by £200 per month?

mocks.simulate-fire-scenario:
FIRE What-If Scenario Comparison
================================

Changes Applied:
  • Monthly Contributions: £1,500.00 → £1,700.00

Baseline (Current Settings):
  FIRE Year: 2038
  FIRE Age: 48
  Days to FIRE: 4,745

Scenario (With Changes):
  FIRE Year: 2037
  FIRE Age: 47
  Days to FIRE: 4,380

Impact:
  FIRE reached 1 year(s) EARLIER (age 47 vs 48).
  365 fewer calendar days to FIRE.

evals.input: @portfolio-tracker What if my growth rate drops to 5%?

mocks.simulate-fire-scenario:
FIRE What-If Scenario Comparison
================================

Changes Applied:
  • Growth Rate: 7.00% → 5.00%

Baseline (Current Settings):
  FIRE Year: 2038
  FIRE Age: 48

Scenario (With Changes):
  FIRE Year: 2042
  FIRE Age: 52

Impact:
  FIRE reached 4 year(s) LATER (age 52 vs 48).

preferences.name: baseCurrency
preferences.title: Base Currency
preferences.description: Currency used to display total portfolio value. All holdings will be converted to this currency.

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
